content.problemStatement:
Chef likes problems involving arrays. Unfortunately, the last one he tried to solve didn't quite get solved.



Chef has an array **A** of **N** positive numbers. He wants to find the number of subarrays for which the sum and product of elements are equal.



Please help Chef find this number.
constraints: The product of all elements of a test case does not exceed 10^9
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of subarrays whose sum of elements is equal to the product of elements.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: subarrays are [1], [3], [2], [1,3,2]. Test case 2: subarrays are [4], [1], [2], [1], [4,1,2,1]. Test case 3: all singletons (6) plus the whole array [1,2,2,2,2,1] where sum=product=10.
Only the five subarrays consisting of a single element have equal sum and product; any longer subarray has product larger than sum.
All four single‑element subarrays satisfy sum=product=1; any longer subarray has sum>product.

hidden.label: edge case with a valid length‑2 subarray
general case with two length‑3 equalities

solution.approach: For each starting index i we extend the subarray to the right while maintaining the current sum and product. Because all numbers are positive, both sum and product grow monotonically, so we can stop early when the product exceeds 10^9 (the given global bound) or when it becomes larger than the sum plus the maximum possible remaining sum, but with N ≤ 50 a simple O(N^2) double loop is fast enough. Every time sum == product we increment the answer. The overall complexity per test case is O(N^2) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, where N ≤ 50, which easily fits the limits.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        a = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        ans = 0
        for i in range(N):
            s = 0
            p = 1
            for j in range(i, N):
                s += a[j]
                p *= a[j]
                if s == p:
                    ans += 1
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
